Alright, welcome back in this video. We're going to talk about what to do when your level three campaigns go wrong. And there's really only two things that can happen when a level three campaign goes wrong. Number one, it doesn't make any money. Or number two, it doesn't spend any money. And we've just had that happen in multiple campaigns. So let's get down to it. Alright, an old stand by, which was this guy? Right here, rather, digital marketing cost cap. This one was a level three campaign I created before making this course. It's been running forever. It made great money. And now, over the last three days, it just isn't. Alright, and I know exactly what's happening. By the way, which is we're spending a lot of money per sale. And I think the reason we're spending a lot of money per sale is because we're paying a lot of money per click. Now, why is that happening? Well, it's because I've been running this ad forever. Alright, so if you remember our little troubleshooting guide that we referenced a couple of videos ago, one of the things is like, alright, if something goes bad, it could be you're just starting to pay a lot more for the traffic and the site isn't actually broken or anything like that. Pretty sure that's what's happening. So if we come over here and we look at this one, alright, so this is the campaign. And again, this is one that we built. I built it before this class and I'm using the old strategy of let's just have a whole bunch of different ad sets, you know, at different bid amounts and let's see what happens here. Our click-through rate is okay. And it's just like our cost per click has gone up a little bit higher than we want it to go. Alright, so that's, you know, what are we going to do there? Well, I'll tell you what we're going to do. We're going to do the old rebuild the campaign from scratch method. Alright, now here's the thing. I'm recording this for you on the 3rd of September. So I know that there's a pretty good chance that over this weekend, ad costs are going to be ridiculously expensive. So here's a little trick, alright. First thing is I'm not going to really change anything. I'm literally, I'm just going to come over here. I'm going to hit duplicate. I'm going to create a new campaign. Alright, and I'm literally copying the exact campaign. Everything as is from before. So this is my daily budget. We're on a cost cap here. Notice it doesn't spend my daily budget. It's just not getting customers at an acceptable cost. It's gotten a little bit funky. That's alright, you know, that happens. What I want to do though, thing number one, and there's really only a couple of things that we have to do in general for this campaign. Thing number one is I'm going to create a start date for this. Alright, so I'm going to come over here and I want all of these guys to actually start on Tuesday, the 7th, and I want to start at midnight. Alright, 8am. Actually, 1220 is fine. I don't care. I just want to let the holiday go by. Alright, because the holiday weekend, I know ad costs are going to be up. So it's like, whatever. There's no point in launching this thing right in the middle of the most competitive advertising season or one of them on Facebook Labor Day weekend. So no worries. I'll just launch it Tuesday morning. So now this is going to automatically get launched on Tuesday morning. Now the next thing that I want to do over here is I just want to make sure, so let's see if I can get lucky. Alright, I just want to make sure that, nope, dang it, they're going to make me do it for each one. I think you know where this is going. I'm just going to turn this off. Okay, so I did it for this one. I did it for this one. I'm not changing my bids. I'm not changing anything. Alright, because this guy's worked and it just kind of stopped. And so the reason I'm doing this is sometimes when you turn a campaign off and then create a new one and wait a couple days and launch the new one, sometimes it just works again. Right out of the gate. Like a lot of times it just works again right out of the gates. It's like, okay, I'll try that. There's no point in reinventing the wheel here. You know, and so that's literally what we're doing. So again, we're ready to go with this thing. I'm going to publish it. Alright, so here we are. You know, everything's cool. Again, like nothing new. I just rebuilt it, duplicated it, made sure it wasn't weird. And now I'm just going to have it where it starts running after Labor Day because I don't want to pay extra.
